body:
Create a table to store F1 Circuit information pulled from the f1api.dev /api/circuits endpoint.

Fields based on API response:
- Circuit ID (string) - unique identifier
- Circuit Name (string)
- Country (string)
- City (string)
- Circuit Length (number) - in kilometres
- Lap Record (string)
- First Participation Year (number)
- Number of Corners (number)
- Fastest Lap Driver ID (string)
- Fastest Lap Team ID (string)
- Fastest Lap Year (number)
- URL (string) - Wikipedia link

Create a subflow that:
1. Calls the Get All Circuits action
2. Processes the JSON output
3. Creates/updates records in the Circuits table
title: Create Circuits table and subflow to populate it from API

body:
I want a table to store the data regarding which Teams were actually active through the various seasons. 

Will be using the endpoint described here:
https://f1api.dev/docs/teams/year-teams

Example response:
{
  "api": "https://f1connectapi.vercel.app",
  "url": "https://f1connectapi.vercel.app/api/2016/teams?limit=1",
  "limit": 1,
  "offset": 0,
  "total": 1,
  "season": "2016",
  "championshipId": "f1_2016",
  "teams": [
    {
      "teamId": "mercedes",
      "teamName": "Mercedes Formula 1 Team",
      "teamNationality": "Germany",
      "firstAppeareance": 1954,
      "constructorsChampionships": 8,
      "driversChampionships": 9,
      "url": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mercedes-Benz_in_Formula_One"
    }
  ]
}
title: Table to store Season Team data

body:
We need a Flow Designer Action that can get data from the Teams endpoint at f1api.dev.

API and endpoint documentation:
https://f1api.dev/docs/teams/teams

The step must output a JSON object back into the flow that utilized the Action.
title: Integrate with Teams endpoint

body:

API and endpoint documentation:
https://f1api.dev/docs/seasons/seasons

The step must output a JSON object back into the flow that utilized the Action.
title: Integrate with Seasons endpoint

body: Revert and move Flow Designer Actions into the F1Now repo. In order to simplify the management of the app.
title: Consolidate two git repos

body:
We need a table to store information about the seasons of Formula 1 up until the current one.  The table should support the data that we can fetch from the seasons endpoint.

Example response from the Seasons endpoint at f1api.dev

{
  "api": "https://f1connectapi.vercel.app",
  "url": "https://f1connectapi.vercel.app/api/seasons?limit=1&offset=0",
  "limit": 1,
  "offset": 0,
  "total": 1,
  "championships": [
    {
      "championshipId": "f1_2025",
      "championshipName": "2025 Formula 1 World Championship",
      "url": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_Formula_One_World_Championship",
      "year": 2025
    }
  ]
}
title: Create a table for tracking seasons (championships)

body: Want to keep an updated list of active F1 teams. Could use the /current/teams endpoint at f1api.dev to identify active teams in the current season, then create some logic to correctly update a true/false field (needs to be created) on the Teams table records.
title: Keep track of active teams

body: Subflow will utilize an Integration Hub Action developed as a part of the F1API-ServiceNow-Spoke app (separate GitHub repo).
title: Subflow that syncs Teams data into a ServiceNow table

body: We are going to need a table to store information about Formula 1 teams. This will be needed when we get the new F1 persons table, so we know which team that person belongs to (reference field). And we need to define the team when we create contracts.
title: Table to store F1 Teams information

body: Since we agreed to start over with a F1 persons table, which will be the parent table for various child tables, like the drivers table, we have to delete the existing drivers table. It can not be converted into a child table to my knowledge.
title: Delete the existing drivers table

body:
As a F1Now application administrator, I need a parent 
table to store information about F1 persons (drivers, 
managers and other team members).

This table will act as a base/parent table that other 
tables extend from, similar to how the ServiceNow 
Task table is extended by Incident, Problem and Change.

Note: Unlike other F1Now tables, this table does NOT 
pull from the https://f1api.dev/docs API. It is a ServiceNow-native 
table created to support the Contract Negotiation 
process (Issue #7).

The existing Drivers table (Issue #5) will be 
refactored to extend from this F1 Persons table.

Proposed Fields:
- First Name (String)
- Last Name (String)
- Nationality (String)  
- Birthday (Date)
- URL (URL)
- Person Type (Choice: Driver, Manager, Team Member)
title: Table to store F1 persons information

body: As a F1 team principal/manager I need to able to start drafting contracts, and approve the proposal so that it's ready to be presented to the driver.
title: Create table for contracts

body: Delete all tables and related components except the Drivers table. So we restart and move forward using GitHub Issues and Projects in a more organized fashion.
title: Reset

body:
As a F1Now application administrator, I need an application table to store information about the Formula 1 drivers.

The table should be able to store the data that is given to us by the drivers API as described on this webpage:
https://f1api.dev/docs/drivers/drivers

{
  "api": "https://f1connectapi.vercel.app",
  "url": "https://f1connectapi.vercel.app/api/drivers?limit=1&offset=20",
  "limit": 1,
  "offset": 20,
  "total": 1,
  "drivers": [
    {
      "driverId": "alonso",
      "name": "Fernando",
      "surname": "Alonso",
      "nationality": "Spain",
      "birthday": "29/07/1981",
      "number": 14,
      "shortName": "ALO",
      "url": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fernando_Alonso"
    }
  ]
}
title: Table to store driver information
